name: Acastus
description: Acastus was the son of Pelias, king of Iolcus, and Anaxibia. He is primarily known for his participation in the Argonautic expedition, where he sailed with Jason in search of the Golden Fleece. Despite the complex and often treacherous relationships within his family, particularly his father's dealings with Jason and his sisters' tragic fate involving Medea, Acastus remained loyal to his companions and played a significant role in navigating the perilous journey. After Pelias was killed by his daughters, tricked by Medea, Acastus avenged his father's death by driving Jason and Medea out of Iolcus. He then became the king of Iolcus himself, ruling with justice and fairness. Later in his reign, he hosted Peleus, who was fleeing after accidentally killing his father-in-law, Eurytion. However, Acastus's wife, Astydameia, fell in love with Peleus and, when rejected, falsely accused him of attempting to seduce her. This led to a series of events orchestrated by Astydameia, including a hunting trip where Peleus was left to die by the centaurs. Peleus eventually survived and, with the help of Jason and the Dioscuri, returned to Iolcus to kill Acastus and Astydameia, reclaiming his honor and avenging the treachery he had suffered. Acastus is remembered as a participant in a grand adventure and a victim of familial and romantic intrigue, highlighting the complexities of heroism and fate in Greek mythology.
subtitle: Argonaut, King of Iolcus, Betrayed Host

keyMyths.title: The Argonautic Expedition
keyMyths.description: Acastus joined Jason and other heroes on the Argo in search of the Golden Fleece. He faced many dangers and challenges alongside his fellow Argonauts, proving his courage and loyalty.

keyMyths.title: Acastus's Revenge
keyMyths.description: After his father, Pelias, was murdered by his own daughters at the instigation of Medea, Acastus drove Jason and Medea out of Iolcus. He became king and sought to restore order and justice to the kingdom.

keyMyths.title: The Treachery of Astydameia
keyMyths.description: Acastus's wife, Astydameia, falsely accused Peleus of seduction, leading to a series of events where Peleus was nearly killed. This act of betrayal eventually led to Acastus's own demise at the hands of Peleus.

sources.author: Apollonius Rhodius
sources.description: A detailed account of the voyage of the Argonauts, including Acastus's role.
sources.text: A detailed account of the voyage of the Argonauts, including Acastus's role.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: Provides information about the myths and legends of Greek heroes, including Acastus and Peleus.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: A comprehensive history that includes accounts of Acastus's reign and his actions after his father's death.
